$description: Component manifest for the Tripletex Atlas component library — designed to be consumed by AI tools (Claude Code, Claude.ai, Figma Make, v0, Lovable, etc.) for prototyping. Pair with tokens/*.css and CLAUDE.md for full context.

Button.description: Primary CTA component for triggering actions with configurable variants and sizes.

variant.description: Visual style: primary=blue bg, secondary=light blue, tertiary=transparent, icon=icon only

ActionButton.description: Button with automatic state indicators (loading, success, error) that manages visual feedback during async actions.

status.description: Action state: shows spinner, checkmark, or warning icon

icon.description: Custom icon (overridden by status icon when status != default)

states.loading: cursor: progress; spinner animation: 0.8s linear infinite

Alert.description: Dismissible inline notification with semantic color variants (info/success/warning/error/automation).

onClose.description: If omitted, close button not shown

role.description: ARIA role; auto-set to alert for error/warning

Avatar.description: Circular profile image or initials badge; supports an online status indicator.

src.description: Image URL; if omitted, shows initials

AvatarGroup.description: Stacked or spaced layout of multiple avatars with overflow indicator showing count of hidden users.

showStatus.description: Spaced layout if true (shows online dots), stacked otherwise

AvatarWithName.description: Avatar paired with user name label, typically used in presence indicators or author bylines.

initials.description: Defaults to first 2 chars of name

Banner.description: Prominent hero message with chip label, title, body, optional illustration or full-bleed media slot, action buttons, decorative background, and close button. Used for Tips / Upsell / Product-Spotlight widgets.

size.description: Fixed widths matching Figma Tips widget: 322/595/1068px

label.description: Chip label; defaults to variant name

media.description: Full-bleed media slot — stacks ABOVE text for small/medium, sits BESIDE text (340px right column) for large. Pair with <MediaPlaceholder /> while waiting on artwork.

decorativeBackground.description: Decorative background circles (set true for Tips/Upsell widget)

Banner.usage: <Banner variant="tips" size="medium" title="Heading" media={<MediaPlaceholder />} primaryAction={{label: 'Button'}} decorativeBackground onClose={() => {}}>Body text</Banner>

MediaPlaceholder.description: Dashed-border empty-image placeholder block (Figma node 223:1231). Use as the `media` slot in Banner while waiting on final artwork.

width.description: Optional fixed width (px or CSS). Defaults to 100%.

MediaPlaceholder.usage: <MediaPlaceholder /> — or pass as media: <Banner variant="tips" media={<MediaPlaceholder />} />

Chip.description: Compact label or filter token with optional icon/avatar; supports static, clickable, and removable variants.

MessageButton.description: Icon button with badge showing unread message count; dot for count=1, pill for count>=2, '9+' for count>9.

CommentButton.description: Icon button with badge for unread comments; toggles between outline and filled icon based on read state.

read.description: If true, badge dark instead of blue; icon switches to filled

CommentView.description: Dedicated panel for displaying, reading, and composing comments with states for empty, loading, comments, and locked.

comments.description: Array of {id?, authorName, authorAvatar?, timestamp, body, unread?}

ContentSwitcher.description: Single-selection segmented control; exactly one option is always selected.
ContentSwitcher.usage: <ContentSwitcher options={[{value: 'a', label: 'Tab A'}, {value: 'b', label: 'Tab B'}]} value={selected} onChange={setSelected} />

MultiContentSwitcher.description: Multi-selection segmented control allowing zero or more options to be active simultaneously.

Label.description: Inline semantic label with icon and background color; used for tagging content with predefined categories.

Input.description: Text input field with optional icon affixes and semantic variants for form data entry.

NumberStepper.description: Numeric input with increment/decrement buttons for controlled value adjustment.

Textarea.description: Multi-line text input with optional resize control and semantic variants.

Select.description: Dropdown select field with options list and semantic variant support.

Period.description: Period display with prev/next navigation buttons for date range or time period selection.

InputGroup.description: Wrapper combining label, control input, and optional helper text for form field grouping.

DecorativeIcon.description: Circular container for displaying an icon with semantic background color and sizing.

Checkbox.description: Single checkbox input with optional label and indeterminate state support.

CheckboxGroup.description: Group container for multiple checkbox inputs with vertical or horizontal layout.

Radio.description: Single radio input with optional label for exclusive selection within a group.

RadioGroup.description: Group container managing multiple radio inputs with automatic exclusive selection and layout options.

options.description: Array of {value, label, disabled?} for auto-generation

Toggle.description: Switch-style input for binary on/off state with optional label.

ToggleGroup.description: Group container for multiple toggle switches with vertical layout and optional label.

Dropdown.description: Searchable dropdown menu for single or multi selection with filterable options and chip display.

Calendar.description: Interactive calendar for selecting a single date, with month navigation, ISO week numbers, and min/max date constraints.

DateRangePicker.description: Dual-calendar date range picker for selecting start and end dates, displaying two adjacent months.

MonthYearPicker.description: Compact month/year selector with year navigation and a 3x4 month grid; supports filled or outlined selection.

PeriodSelector.description: Timeline-based period selector with horizontal scrolling rows (year/quarters/months/weeks), quick-range buttons and confirm.

Modal.description: Dialog with optional illustration, title, subtitle, content area, and primary/secondary actions; supports desktop and mobile (bottom-sheet) variants.

onClose.description: Called on Esc, backdrop click, or close button

Modal.usage: <Modal isOpen={true} title="Success" primaryAction={{label: 'Continue', onClick: handleContinue}} onClose={handleClose} />

SuccessIllustration.description: Decorative SVG illustration for success-themed modals, featuring geometric shapes in Tripletex colors.

Pagination.description: Simple pagination with Previous/Next buttons and centered page number display.

TablePagination.description: Table-optimized pagination displaying item range (e.g. '1-10 of 45') with icon navigation buttons.

Popover.description: Informational popover with title, body, optional link, label chip, and directional arrow pointing to its trigger.
Popover.usage: <Popover title="Info" body="This is helpful context" arrow="bottom" />

PopoverOpener.description: Interactive label with an info icon that opens/triggers a popover; sizes and weights are customizable.

PopupMenu.description: Context menu container with vertically stacked menu items and optional group headers/dividers.

PopupMenuItem.description: Individual menu item for use within PopupMenu, supporting icon, selection state, focus ring, and disabled state.

PopupGroupHeader.description: Separator and optional label for grouping menu items in a PopupMenu, with optional divider line.

Spinner.description: Rotating multi-color loading spinner with configurable size and speed; optionally displays a label.

InlineSpinner.description: Compact inline spinner variant for buttons or inline text; defaults to tiny size with 'Loading' label.

ModifierKey.description: SVG glyph for a keyboard modifier key (Cmd/Option/Shift/etc.), with platform-specific (Mac/Win) variants.

Shortcut.description: Keyboard shortcut display combining one or more modifier keys plus an optional letter, with visual separation.

Tooltip.description: Single-line tooltip with label and optional keyboard shortcut, displayed with a triangular arrow pointing to the trigger.

MultilineTooltip.description: Multi-line tooltip with bold title, description text, optional shortcut, and customizable max-width.
MultilineTooltip.usage: <MultilineTooltip title="Keyboard Shortcut" description="Press Cmd+S to save your work" shortcut={<Shortcut keys={['primary']} letter="s" />} />

Topbar.description: Top navigation bar with menu, logo, company picker, search, assistant/favorites/notifications/messages icons, and profile avatar.

AppShell.description: Top-level layout component wrapping topbar, sidebar, and main content area.

Sidebar.description: Vertical navigation panel with icon-labeled items; supports collapse mode (compact icon-only).

items.description: Array of {label, icon, subItems?}

SidebarHeader.description: Expandable navigation item with optional submenu in the sidebar.

SidebarItem.description: Submenu item within an expanded SidebarHeader.

SubitemIcons.description: Visual indicator for sidebar submenu state (dot, mini-dot, expandable/expanded chevron).

Status.description: Inline status badge with colored dot or count and label.

count.description: If provided, shows count badge instead of dot

Table.description: Data table with sorting, selection (checkboxes), row expansion, and tone-based row coloring.

columns.description: Array of {id, label, align?, sortable?, width?, render?}

Table.usage: <Table columns={[{id: 'name', label: 'Name', sortable: true}]} rows={data} onSortChange={setSort} />

TableView.description: Settings panel for table density (compact/standard/detailed) and column visibility toggling.

TableFilter.description: Toolbar combining filter button, search input, and action buttons for tables.

FilterButton.description: Toggle button for filter panel with optional count badge for active filters.

TableFilterIconButton.description: Icon button for table toolbar (column settings, print, refresh, etc.).

FilterDialog.description: Modal/dropdown dialog containing filter controls, saved filter list, and save/reset actions.

SavedFilterItem.description: Individual saved filter entry with name and menu trigger button.

ProgressStepper.description: Multi-step progress indicator with vertical or horizontal orientation; supports bullet/number/icon styles.

PageHeader.description: Page title row with breadcrumb, autosave status, status marker, subheading, and primary/secondary action buttons.
PageHeader.import: import { PageHeader, Breadcrumb, Autosave, StatusMarker } from "./components";

Autosave.description: Autosave status indicator with cloud icon (saved or saving state).

label.description: Custom label; defaults to state text

StatusMarker.description: Inline status indicator with colored dot and label (warning or success variants).

Icon.description: Individual SVG icon, exported as <KebabName>Icon (e.g. ChevronDownIcon, SearchIcon). Three sizes available: 20, 24, 48px.
Icon.import: import { ChevronDownIcon, SearchIcon } from "./components"; // or: import { Icons } from "./components"; const Cmp = Icons["chevron-down"];

aria-label.description: Required if icon is semantic (not decorative)

icons.$description: Icon library — each name resolves to an exported component <NameInPascalCase>Icon, plus a dynamic Icons[name] map. Sizes: 20, 24, 48px.
icons.componentNamingRule: kebab-case → PascalCase + 'Icon' suffix. Examples: 'chevron-down' → ChevronDownIcon, 'arrow-left' → ArrowLeftIcon, 'check-circle' → CheckCircleIcon, 'magic-wand' → MagicWandIcon.

sidebarIcons.$description: Brand-specific colored icons used ONLY in the Tripletex left sidebar. These are different from the regular icon library: 32×32 viewBox (vs. 20/24/48), each carries a baked-in brand color (NOT currentColor), and each maps to a top-level navigation route. Use these — not the generic monochrome icons — when prototyping the Tripletex sidebar so the visual identity is correct.
sidebarIcons.componentNamingRule: kebab-case → 'BrandSidebar' + PascalCase + 'Icon'. Special cases: 'lønn' → 'lonn' (BrandSidebarLonnIcon), 'årsoppgjør' → 'arsoppgjor' (BrandSidebarArsoppgjorIcon). Examples: 'hjem' → BrandSidebarHjemIcon, 'reiser-og-utlegg' → BrandSidebarReiserOgUtleggIcon.
sidebarIcons.selectionPattern: When an item is selected the sidebar wrapper applies a blue background pill (--action-primary-rest #0a41fa). The icon stays its brand color OR is overridden to white via the `color` prop on the icon component — depending on contrast. The brand-color icons themselves do NOT have a separate selected variant.

illustrations.$description: Decorative SVGs for empty states, success screens, onboarding, and confirmations. Located in assets/illustrations/.
illustrations.usage: or import as React component

items.name: celebration
items.description: Confetti and balloons; for milestones, achievements, sign-up completions, account upgrades.

items.name: cloud-check
items.description: Cloud with checkmark; for sync confirmation, save success, backup completed states.

items.name: coins
items.description: Stacked coins; for finance-related screens, transactions, payments, savings.

items.name: error
items.description: Error/warning visual; for failure states, broken pages, critical errors.

items.name: ghost-sad
items.description: Sad ghost; for 404 pages, not-found states, deleted item screens.

items.name: ghost
items.description: Friendly ghost; for empty states, no-data screens, light onboarding.

items.name: reject
items.description: Rejection/blocked visual; for declined actions, denied access, invalid input.

items.name: rocket
items.description: Rocket launching; for getting-started screens, new feature intros, onboarding.

items.name: send
items.description: Paper airplane; for submission confirmations, message sent, email delivered.

items.name: success
items.description: Success checkmark visual; for completed actions, payment success, task done.

items.name: trash
items.description: Trash/wastebin; for deletion confirmations, archive screens.

items.name: user
items.description: Generic user silhouette; placeholder avatar, profile screens, no-photo fallback.
